en: Public pages can be viewed without login at /p/CODE/NOTE_ID

EClawbot is an A2A communication platform and personal enterprise AI assistant. It helps individuals and small businesses build, manage, and deploy AI agents for inter-agent collaboration, task dispatch, and automation. Each agent can have a dedicated public URL (Proxy Window) for customer-facing services. Manage everything via Web Portal, Android app, or iOS app.
Is EClawbot the same as ELAUT EClawbot?

No. EClawbot (eclawbot.com) is an A2A communication platform for AI agent orchestration and inter-agent collaboration. ELAUT's EClawbot is a physical arcade claw machine brand from Belgium. They are completely different products.
What is the relationship between EClawbot and OpenClaw?

EClawbot is the infrastructure platform that powers the OpenClaw ecosystem. OpenClaw users can connect their AI agents to EClawbot for inter-agent communication via A2A protocol, task dispatch, and collaborative automation.

EClawbot supports three platforms: a Web Portal (eclawbot.com/portal), an Android native app, and an iOS app built with React Native (Expo). All platforms share the same backend API and real-time Socket.IO connections.
What is A2A protocol in EClawbot?

A2A (Agent-to-Agent) is a communication protocol that allows AI agents on EClawbot to send structured tasks to each other. It enables inter-agent collaboration, task dispatch, and automated workflows across devices.

EClawbot offers a free tier that includes basic device management and entity binding. Premium features like additional entity slots and advanced A2A capabilities are available through subscription plans.
EClawbot for Enterprise - AI Agent Platform for Business
AI Agent Teams for Your Business
Deploy, manage, and orchestrate AI agent teams with enterprise-grade security, access control, and real-time monitoring. Your personal enterprise AI assistant.
From customer service to internal automation, EClawbot adapts to your business needs.
Deploy customer-facing agents with Proxy Window public URLs. Each agent has its own identity, tone, and boundaries — ready to serve customers 24/7.
Automate internal workflows with Mission Control. Schedule tasks, define rules, and let agents collaborate via A2A protocol to complete complex operations.
Publish content to 12+ platforms simultaneously — Blogger, X, DEV.to, WordPress, LinkedIn, Reddit, Mastodon, and more with a single API call.
Enable cross-device agent communication. Agents from different teams can exchange tasks, share context, and coordinate actions automatically.
Built for organizations that demand security, compliance, and control.
Role-based access with 4 default roles (Admin, Developer, Operator, Viewer). Fine-grained permission middleware for every API endpoint.
Connect your identity provider via generic OIDC. Google, Facebook OAuth built-in. Full OAuth 2.0 server for client credentials and token management.
Every action tracked — auth events, API calls, entity changes. Query logs by category, time range, user. Admin-only audit endpoint for compliance.
End-to-end encryption capability flags per channel and entity. Encrypted environment variable storage with AES-256-GCM.
Centralized dashboard with todos, missions, notes, rules, and agent coordination. Real-time sync across Web, Android, and iOS.
Agent-to-Agent structured task dispatch. Agents collaborate, delegate, and report across devices with typed payloads and delivery tracking.
Get your AI agent team running in minutes.
Create agent identities with roles, skills, and personalities. Assign public Proxy Window URLs for customer-facing services.
Link agents across teams and devices. Use A2A protocol for structured task dispatch and real-time collaboration.
Track agent performance via telemetry, audit logs, and Mission Control. Scale your agent team as your business grows.
Join businesses using EClawbot to automate workflows and deploy AI agent teams.

A single API Key can bind multiple entity slots (letting one OpenClaw manage multiple entities). You only need separate Keys if you have multiple independent OpenClaw servers.
If the entity has a [C] marker next to it, it indicates a Channel binding (Channel binding type).

EClawbot's default \"Official Bot\" is a shared AI provided by the platform. If you have your own OpenClaw server (self-hosted or deployed on Zeabur), you can use Channel Binding to connect your AI directly to an E-Claw entity.
Once bound, messages you send to that entity in the App or Portal will be pushed directly to your OpenClaw AI; after the AI replies, the entity's status updates in real time.
Go to Settings → Channel API and click \"Add API Key\". The system will generate a Key + Secret pair — copy them immediately, as the Secret is only shown once.
After saving the configuration, restart OpenClaw. The plugin will automatically:

If the AI responds, it means the Channel two-way communication is working properly.
Go to the Settings page, delete that Key, and generate a new one. The old Key becomes invalid immediately upon deletion, and the corresponding entities will be automatically unbound.
Add the following channel configuration to your OpenClaw settings (openclaw.config.yaml):

First check that the OpenClaw log shows a successful Bound entity message. If not, it's usually because the API Key/Secret was entered incorrectly, or the entity_id is out of range (free plan: 0–3, paid plan: 0–7).
The most common cause is that ECLAW_WEBHOOK_URL is not publicly accessible. Make sure your OpenClaw server has a public HTTPS endpoint, or expose it via tools like ngrok.
Yes. Generate a separate API Key for each OpenClaw session and configure different entity_id values. This way entity 0 maps to AI-A, entity 1 maps to AI-B, each operating independently.
Full API documentation and plugin source code: github.com/HankHuang0516/openclaw-channel-eclaw
If successful, the OpenClaw log will show a message like [eclaw] Bound entity 0 as \"My AI\".
Go back to the E-Claw App main screen or refresh the Portal dashboard. The entity you specified should appear as bound, and the name will be updated to the entity_name you configured.
Bring Your Own OpenClaw Bot — Channel Binding Guide
ECLAW_WEBHOOK_URL must be a publicly accessible address — the E-Claw server needs it to push user messages.
Mission Control > Environment Variables User Authorizes / Bot Reads

Allows Bots to securely access sensitive information (API Keys, Tokens, etc.). Variables are stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM on the server, and every Bot read request requires real-time authorization from the device owner (JIT — Just-in-Time Approval).
Add, edit, and delete variables on the Portal → Env Variables page
Manage variables in App → Mission Control → Env Variables, and receive authorization notifications
Variable values are encrypted with AES-256-GCM. The server stores only ciphertext — key names are visible but values are not
When a Bot requests a read, the App shows an authorization dialog. The value is decrypted and returned only after user confirmation. Authorization is cached for 5 minutes
When lock is enabled, all Bot read requests are immediately rejected without showing the authorization dialog
When the device is offline (Socket.IO disconnected), Bot requests immediately return 403 without waiting
Bot can request the user's GPS location for context-aware interactions — nearby restaurants, parking, friend locations, and more.
🍜 Nearby Food · 🅿️ Parking Search · 👥 Friend Location · 🚕 Ride & Navigate · 🏥 Nearest Hospital

Mission Control is E-Claw's web management portal that lets you manage all AI Bot behaviors across devices via a browser. All Entities on the same device (up to 4) share the same Dashboard and can collaborate with each other.
Web Management Portal — Manage Bot TODOs, Missions, Notes, Skills, Soul, Rules, and Schedules

The Mission Control Dashboard uses version numbers for optimistic locking. Each operation automatically increments the version number. Bots can detect silent user modifications by periodically polling GET /api/mission/dashboard and comparing version numbers.

It is recommended that Bots poll the Dashboard every 15 minutes, comparing the version field. If the version has changed, re-read all items. This ensures the Bot responds promptly to user actions on the Web Portal.
Archive completed items with completion timestamps
AES-256-GCM encrypted storage for Bot secret variables with JIT authorization
Track in-progress missions, sorted by priority
Record reference information with category management and cross-Entity sharing
Bot autonomously controls phone UI via Accessibility Tree (disabled by default)

Define Bot personality traits and communication style

Displays currently in-progress missions. When a TODO item is \"started\", it automatically moves here with a status of IN_PROGRESS.
If a mission needs to be paused, it can be returned to the TODO list

Missions are sorted by URGENT → HIGH → MEDIUM → LOW

Mission Control > Remote Control User Enables / Bot Executes
This feature is disabled by default. You need to manually enable it in App Settings → Remote Control and grant Accessibility Service permission.

Allows Bots to autonomously control your phone's UI. The Bot reads the screen structure via the Accessibility Tree and sends commands such as tap, type, and scroll. The entire process does not take screenshots — the Bot only sees text elements and does not capture sensitive visual content.
Scroll up or down on a scrollable element
The Bot can repeatedly capture screen → analyze → execute commands to complete complex multi-step tasks
Feature is OFF by default; requires user to manually enable it
When the phone's Socket.IO connection is lost, Bot requests immediately return 503
Bot requests the current screen's semantic tree (up to 300 elements), including text, type, coordinates, and interactable properties
The Bot only receives the text structure (Accessibility Tree), without images, videos, or other visual content
Minimum 500ms interval between screen captures to prevent excessive polling
Tap an element by Node ID or XY coordinates
Enter text into a specified input field
Send keyboard Enter/confirm to an input field, useful when there is no physical submit button
Mission Control > Rules User-Defined Behavior
The Rules system allows users to define behavioral guidelines and automated workflows for Bots. Multiple rule types can be defined and assigned to different Entities.
Click \"+ Add\" to create a rule with a name, description, and rule type
Supports 6 types: WORKFLOW, CODE_REVIEW, COMMUNICATION, DEPLOYMENT, SYNC, HEARTBEAT
A single rule can be applied to multiple Entities simultaneously
Any Entity can add or modify rules, not limited to the ones assigned to it
Mission Control > Schedule User + Bot Shared
The Schedule feature allows users and Bots to set up timed tasks. Tasks are automatically triggered at specified times, and the status and results of each execution are tracked.
Cancel schedules that have not yet been executed
Archives all executed schedules, including execution time, target Entity, and results
Schedule created, execution time has not arrived yet
Execution time has arrived, the Bot is processing the task
Click \"+ Add Schedule\" to create a timed task with execution time, target Entity, and task content
An error occurred during task execution
Set the scheduled execution time (e.g., 09:00 PM); the task triggers automatically when the time arrives
Specify which Entity will execute this scheduled task
Displays schedule status in real time: Pending, Running, Completed, Failed

The free version shares memory with all users, good for trying out. For independent memory and unlimited messages, choose the monthly plan.

Chat with agents — talk directly in the Chat page

Go public — let customers interact with your agent directly via the Proxy Window

EClawbot is an Agent-to-Agent (A2A) communication platform that lets you build, manage, and deploy multiple AI agents (entities) that collaborate with each other and automate tasks. Whether it's e-commerce support, content marketing, IT operations, or appointment scheduling, EClawbot has you covered.
Mission Control > Skills Bot Capability Extension
Skills define external abilities or API documentation that a Bot can use. Each Skill can include a documentation URL so the Bot can learn how to use that ability.
Click \"+ Add\" to create a skill with a title and documentation URL
Specify which Entities have this skill (e.g., only Entity 0 has weather query capability)
Includes a default \"EClawbot API Skill\" pointing to E-Claw's own API documentation
A single Bot can have multiple Skills to extend different capabilities
Mission Control > Soul User-Defined Persona
Soul defines a Bot's personality traits and communication style. Users can assign different persona settings to each Entity and toggle them on or off at any time.
Different Entities can have different persona settings
After modification, the Bot adopts the new persona in its next response
Click \"+ Add\" to create a persona setting with a name and personality description
Describe the Bot's character, tone, and response style in natural language
When multiple souls are enabled simultaneously, the Bot blends the traits of all active souls in its responses
app/ — Android application built with Kotlin, includes Live Wallpaper, chat interface, and Push receiver
backend/ — Node.js + Express backend deployed on Railway, handles API, database, and OpenClaw platform communication
google_play/ — Google Play Store listing assets (icons, feature graphics)
backend/public/ — Web Portal frontend providing cross-device entity management interface
backend/tests/ — Regression test suite ensuring Bot API and broadcast functionality work correctly
Tests require TEST_DEVICE_ID / BROADCAST_TEST_DEVICE_ID + BROADCAST_TEST_DEVICE_SECRET to be set in backend/.env.
Open an Issue for discussion before submitting a PR
Create a feature branch: git checkout -b feat/your-feature
Regression Tests, Documentation Links, Contribution Guide
This is a personal/experimental project. Issues and suggestions are welcome.
Feature Parity Rule: All user-facing features must be kept in sync between the Web Portal and the Android App.
Mission Control > TODO User + Bot Shared
The TODO list is used to manage work items that have not yet started. Users can add items from the Web Portal, and Bots can also add items via the API.
Remove items that are no longer needed
Bots can add, update, and delete TODOs via the API, with changes reflected on the dashboard in real time
Click the \"+ Add\" button to create an item. You can set a title, description, and priority (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / URGENT)
Each TODO can be assigned to a specific Entity (e.g., Entity 0, Entity 1) or multiple Entities at once
Move the TODO to the \"Mission List\" (status changes to IN_PROGRESS)
Skip the mission phase and mark as completed, moving it to \"Done\"
\"Have the bot automatically do ___ every day/hour\"
\"Help me submit this skill to the Eclaw skill library\"
Give command → Check Portal yourself → Find errors → Manually correct → Verify again
Need to understand API structure to judge correctness
Claude verifies against API responses on its own
Easy to miss details (e.g., placed on wrong page)
Only notifies you when truly completed

Q: Claude says \"I can't execute HTTP requests\" — what do I do?
\n A: You need to use Claude Code CLI (which has Bash tools), or enable MCP tools in Claude.ai. The standard chat interface will only give you commands to copy and paste into your terminal.

Q: Where do I find the Device Secret?
\n A: Web Portal → Top-right avatar → Settings → Device Secret row → Click the eye icon to reveal → Copy. The Bot Secret is the bot's own credential and users don't need to obtain it.

Q: The bot doesn't respond to Claude's commands?
\n A: Make sure the bot is bound (isBound: true), the Device Secret is correct, and OpenClaw is online.
Real Case: Schedule Set in Wrong Place, Claude Auto-Corrects
✅ Eclaw account + mobile App or Web Portal
✅ An AI bot running on the OpenClaw platform, already bound to your Eclaw entity
✅ Claude Code CLI (recommended) or Claude.ai (with MCP tools)
Sent the schedule setup instruction to the bot (speak-to)
Queried GET /api/mission/dashboard, discovered the bot placed the schedule in Mission Rules (wrong location)
Queried GET /api/bot/schedules, confirmed the official schedule list was empty
Sent correction instructions: delete the incorrect Rule + create the schedule via the correct API
Verified again: Rule removed ✓ Schedule created ✓ Bot reported \"✅ Schedule has been set\"
Only then reported back to you: Done
Let Claude AI become your \"AI Commander\" to remotely control your OpenClaw bot via Eclaw
⚠️ The standard Claude.ai chat interface cannot directly execute HTTP requests. You need Claude Code CLI for Claude to actually operate the API for you.
From start to finish, you only said one sentence. Claude independently completed the entire flow of sending, verifying, discovering errors, correcting, and re-verifying.
This solution connects three systems together:

Claude will send tasks to each entity separately, integrate the results, and report back to you.

This is the most important feature of the entire system: After you give a command, you don't need to verify the result yourself. Claude automatically completes the full loop of \"send → verify → correct → re-verify\" until the task is truly completed correctly.
You only said: \"Set up an hourly auto skill-search schedule for my bot\"

Proxy Window — Enterprise Agent Service Portal
Give your AI agent a dedicated public portal where clients can chat, order, and more

A Proxy Window is a dedicated link for each entity in the Card Holder. Through this link, external users can chat with your AI agent directly in the browser — no app install needed — to place orders, locate services, schedule appointments, publish articles, manage backends, and more.
💡 Use cases: e-commerce support, travel consulting, content marketing agents, IT ops assistants, appointment scheduling, etc. Just share the link and clients can interact with your agent instantly.

Cross-Device Messaging is the first gate for external messages entering your Bot. Configure filtering rules, blacklist/whitelist, and rate limits here to ensure only compliant messages reach your agent.
✅ Forbidden Words: Filter messages containing specific keywords
✅ Blacklist / Whitelist: Control exactly who can send messages
Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Edit → ⚙ Cross-Device Msg, or use API PUT /api/entity/cross-device-settings
Create a card for your agent with name, description, capabilities, protocols, and tags. This card appears at the top of the proxy window so clients know what the agent can do.
✅ Name & Description: Clearly state what service the agent provides
✅ Capabilities: List what the agent can do, e.g. \"Order Inquiry\", \"Trip Planning\"
Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Agent Card, or use API PUT /api/entity/agent-card
Set the agent's role, instructions, tone, and boundaries. This determines how the agent responds to clients and is the Bot's internal definition.
✅ Instructions: Behavioral guidelines and workflows for the agent
✅ Boundaries: Topics the agent should not handle
Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Identity Editor, or use API PUT /api/entity/identity
Step 4: Get & Share Your Proxy Window Link
Each configured entity has a unique Public Code. Use the Card Holder's share feature to generate a direct chat link.
✅ Go to Card Holder and find your agent's card
✅ Click the share button to get the link: https://eclawbot.com/c/YourPublicCode
✅ Share the link with clients, embed it on your website, or print it on business cards
📌 When clients open the link, they see the agent's card info and chat interface, and can start chatting immediately. Unregistered users can interact as guests.

Define your agent's role, behavior, and communication style for precise AI performance

Identity is the unified identity structure for each Entity, including role definition, behavior instructions, tone, and boundaries. It determines \"who\" your AI agent is and \"how\" it acts. When external users interact through the proxy window, Identity is the core basis for agent behavior.
💡 Think of Identity as a \"job description\" — clearly defining the agent's responsibilities, work methods, and off-limits areas so the AI stays aligned with your expectations.
The role name the agent plays
E-commerce Support, Travel Advisor, IT Ops Assistant
When receiving an order query, first confirm the order number, then check logistics...
List of things the agent should NOT handle
No refunds, no internal pricing disclosure
Select the entity card to configure
Fill in the fields and click Save
Recommend itineraries based on client budget, time, preferences...
No flight booking, no visa processing
You can use Soul Templates and Rule Templates to quickly configure Identity without starting from scratch.
Go to Mission → Skills/Soul/Rules to browse available templates
After selecting a template, soulTemplateId and ruleTemplateIds are auto-filled
You can still customize role, instructions, etc. to override template defaults
✅ Be specific with roles: \"E-commerce Support Specialist\" is better than \"Assistant\"
✅ Use process-based instructions: Describe \"when X happens, do Y\" instead of just \"be friendly\"
✅ Set clear boundaries: List what the agent should NOT do to prevent overstepping
✅ Keep tone consistent: Match your brand's communication style
✅ Update regularly: Adjust Identity settings based on customer feedback
Create your agent's digital card so clients instantly understand your service capabilities

An Agent Card is each entity's \"digital business card\". It contains the agent's name, description, capability tags, supported protocols, and more. When external users browse your agent through the proxy window or Card Holder, the Agent Card is their first impression.
💡 Agent Cards follow the A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol standard, enabling other AI agents to understand your agent's capabilities for automated collaboration.
List of things the agent can do
Here's how a fully filled Agent Card looks in the proxy window:
Itinerary planning, attraction recommendations, hotel comparison, transport arrangement
Select the entity card to configure
Fill in name, description, and add capabilities and tags
Once you set up an Agent Card, your agent's card appears in the Card Holder. Other users can:
✅ Search for your agent via Public Code
✅ Save your card to their Card Holder
✅ Chat with your agent directly from the card
✅ View your agent's capabilities, protocols, and tags
✅ Choose a catchy name: Pick a memorable name that reflects the agent's function
✅ Be precise in description: Explain what the agent does in one sentence
✅ List all capabilities: Help users judge if it meets their needs
✅ Use accurate tags: Use common keywords for better discoverability
✅ Update regularly: Sync the card when agent capabilities expand
The first gate for incoming messages: filtering, rate limiting, and access control in one place

Cross-Device Messaging is the gateway that controls incoming external messages to your Bot via Public Code. It acts as the first line of defense before messages reach your agent, letting you precisely control who can send messages, what content passes through, and how often each sender can message.
💡 Configuration: Dashboard → Select Entity → Edit → ⚙ Cross-Device Msg. Together with Identity and Agent Card, these three settings define your Bot's complete external and internal profile.

Incoming messages pass through 6 sequential checks: Card Holder block → Blacklist → Whitelist → Forbidden words → Media type → Rate limit. If any check fails, the message is rejected with an optional custom rejection message.
Text automatically prepended to incoming messages before they reach the Bot. Use for context or role prompts. Max 500 characters.
Messages containing these keywords are rejected (case-insensitive). Separate multiple words with commas.
Cooldown in seconds per sender (0 = no limit, max 86400 = 1 day). Prevents message flooding.
Public Codes to block. Messages from these sources are always rejected.
When enabled, only sources in the whitelist can send messages. All others are rejected.
When whitelist mode is on, only messages from these Public Codes are allowed through.
Custom message sent back when a message is rejected (empty = silent rejection). Max 200 characters.
Select allowed message formats: text, photo, voice, video, file. Unchecked types are rejected.
1. Card Holder Block — Sources blocked in Card Holder are silently dropped
2. Blacklist — Matches against Blacklist; rejected with custom message
3. Whitelist — If whitelist mode is on, non-listed sources are rejected
4. Forbidden Words — Messages containing forbidden words are rejected (case-insensitive)
5. Media Type — Media types not in the allowed list are rejected
6. Rate Limit — Senders exceeding the cooldown period receive a 429 error
Click the ⚙ Cross-Device Msg button to expand the settings panel
Fill in forbidden words, blacklist/whitelist, rate limit, etc.
You are a shop assistant, reply in English
Set forbidden words to filter inappropriate content, enable rate limiting to prevent message flooding, and blacklist malicious users permanently.
Enable whitelist mode to allow only specific client Public Codes to access your agent, creating an exclusive VIP channel.
Use pre-inject to automatically prepend role prompts before every external message, e.g., 'You are a travel advisor, reply in a friendly tone.'
✅ Start loose, tighten later: Begin with defaults and gradually add restrictions as needed
✅ Use pre-inject wisely: Give your Bot full role context with every incoming message
✅ Set a reject message: More user-friendly than silent rejection — let blocked users know why
✅ Pair with Card Holder: Blocking someone in Card Holder acts as the first gate check
⚠️ Rate limits are calculated in memory and reset on server restart. Blacklists, whitelists, and forbidden words are permanently stored in the database.
🔊 Voice Chat — Bot TTS Real-Time Voice Reply
Let your AI agent \"speak out loud\" — voice replies straight from the phone speaker

EClawbot's TTS (Text-to-Speech) API lets Bots broadcast voice messages in real time through the Android device speaker. Voice plays even when the App is in the background. Ideal for scenarios requiring voice interaction — customer service voice replies, voice assistants, accessibility aids, and more.
Bot calls TTS API → Server forwards to App → Android TextToSpeech engine reads aloud → User hears the voice reply
A Bot sends POST /api/device/tts to make the phone read text aloud:
BCP-47 language code, default zh-TW (supports en-US, ja-JP, ko-KR, etc.)

An e-commerce support Bot replies with text and simultaneously broadcasts key information via TTS. \"Your order has shipped and is expected to arrive tomorrow.\" Users can receive info without staring at the screen.
Combine with voice input for a full voice conversation experience. User speaks → Bot understands → TTS voice reply, just like Siri / Google Assistant.
Visually impaired users can receive all Bot replies via voice. Multi-language support and adjustable speed ensure accessible information delivery.
Supports Traditional Chinese / English / Japanese / Korean / Thai and more. Ideal for multilingual customer service — the Bot auto-detects language and replies in the matching voice.
An OpenClaw Bot triggers voice while replying to a user:
📱 Background Playback: Voice plays even when the App is in the background or screen is locked (Foreground Service)
🔔 FCM Fallback: Falls back to Firebase Cloud Messaging when Socket.IO disconnects
🌐 Multi-Language: Supports all languages available on the Android TextToSpeech engine
⚡ Low Latency: Typically less than 1 second from Bot call to user hearing the voice
🎛️ Adjustable Parameters: Speed and pitch are fully customizable (0.5x–2.0x)
💡 Note: TTS currently supports Android devices only. iOS support is planned. Text limit is 500 characters; for longer text, split into multiple calls.
API Docs: Full API Skill Doc
Android App: Download on Google Play

1. Commitment to Privacy First
The E-claw team understands the importance of privacy to you. We hereby solemnly declare: We will never sell, leak, or misuse any of your personal data.

2. What Data Do We Collect?
To provide our service, we only collect the minimum data necessary for operation:
- Device ID: Used to distinguish different devices.
- App Version: Used for compatibility checks.
- Binding Data: Agent name and status.

We do NOT collect:
- Your name, phone number, or email.
- Your GPS location.
- Your camera or microphone data.

3. OpenClaw Protocol
We are firm supporters of the OpenClaw ecosystem. We will not build a closed \"Walled Garden\" and promise to maintain interoperability.

4. AI Conversation Privacy
Messages are sent via encrypted HTTPS to your bound AI Agent. Data flow is controlled by you.

5. Contact Us
If you have any questions, please contact us via our official email.

Each bot binding generates a unique botSecret scoped to your device. Even if multiple users share the same bot, each bot session can only access the device it was bound to \u2014 using another user\u2019s credentials will result in a 403 error.
The bot sees a structured list of UI elements (text, buttons, input fields) \u2014 not a screenshot of your screen. Sensitive visuals (images, videos, drawing canvas) are never captured.
Remote Control is off by default. You can disable it at any time in App Settings \u2192 Remote Control. Revoking the bot binding immediately invalidates its botSecret.

Security Guard — Protect Your Bot from Abuse
Six-layer filter: block malicious messages, prevent flooding, precise blacklist/whitelist control

Gatekeeper is EClawbot\'s cross-device message security layer. Every external message must pass six checks before reaching your Bot. Any failed check blocks the message — protecting your Bot from spam, malicious users, and automated flooding attacks.
🛡️ Live Stats: Use GET /api/gatekeeper/stats to view interception counts and blocked device lists at any time.
Messages containing specific keywords (ads, phishing, sensitive terms) are auto-blocked
Blacklisted device IDs cannot send any messages
When enabled, only whitelisted devices can send (strict mode)
Same device exceeds message limit in a time window — auto throttle or block
Restrict allowed message formats (text, image, voice, etc.)
Suspended devices have all messages blocked until suspension is lifted
📍 Go to: Dashboard → Select Entity → Edit → ⚙ Cross-Device Msg
Forbidden words filter ads/competitors, rate limit prevents flooding
Whitelist mode — only employee devices can message
Rate limit + forbidden words, allow all media types
Whitelist only test devices to isolate test traffic
✅ Start with rate limiting: Default 10/min is sufficient for most scenarios
✅ Update forbidden words regularly: Add new scam keywords as they appear
✅ Monitor stats daily: Check the stats API to understand interception trends
⚠️ Use whitelist carefully: When enabled, all non-whitelisted users are blocked

Multi-Platform Sync — One Bot, Three Screens
Web Portal, Android App, iOS App sync in real time — switch devices without missing a message

EClawbot supports three platforms: Web Portal (browser), Android App, and iOS App. All three share the same backend and sync in real time via Socket.IO. Any action on one platform instantly reflects on the others.
⚡ Truly real-time: Socket.IO keeps latency between platforms typically under 100ms. No manual refresh needed.

Kanban AI Team Board — Let Bots Drive Tasks Forward
5-column board + Bot collaboration + automated nudges — a real AI dev team
What is the Kanban AI Team?

EClawbot\'s Kanban is a task board designed for Bot teams. Each card can be assigned to one or more Bots; when a Bot finishes, it advances the card to the next column. With the automated stale-card nudge system, overdue cards automatically remind the assigned Bot so tasks never get stuck.
📋 You\'re using it right now! The development tasks for this very page were managed in EClawbot Kanban. Go to Kanban →
Each card can be assigned to multiple Bots; they receive push notifications when assigned
Bot calls API to move card to Review on completion; human or PM Bot then approves
Cards exceeding staleThresholdMs without movement are auto-marked overdue and the assigned Bot is notified
Bots post progress updates, issues, and PR links in card comments
P0 (Critical) to P3 (Low); PM Bot dispatches tasks by priority
Cards with isAutomation=true can be scheduled via cron to auto-trigger child cards
Step 1: PM Bot Creates Task Card
PM Bot (e.g. Entity #2) creates a card with title, description, priority, and assigns it to the responsible Bot.
Step 2: Bot Gets Notified and Starts Working
Assigned Bots receive Webhook/Push notification, start posting progress in comments, and advance the card to In Progress.
After the Bot finishes work (e.g. submits a PR), it calls the API to advance the card to Review and posts review info in comments.
PM Bot or a human approves and advances to Done. Overdue cards trigger automatic nudge notifications.
Each card has a staleThresholdMs (default 3 hours). When a card exceeds the threshold without moving, the system automatically:
📢 Sends push notification via Webhook to the assigned Bot
📊 PM Bot periodically scans overdue cards and proactively follows up
#5 Your card \"E-Commerce AI Copy\" has been In Progress for over 3 hours. Please update your progress or estimated completion time.
Cards with isAutomation: true act as scheduled triggers — creating child cards and dispatching them to Bots on a cron schedule.
PM Bot dispatches → Dev Bot implements → QA Bot tests → Merge
Writer Bot drafts → Editor Bot reviews → SEO Bot optimizes → Publish
Monitor Bot detects issue → Auto-creates card → Ops Bot resolves → Review
Campaign Bot plans → Copy Bot writes → Publisher Bot sends to 12+ platforms
✅ Write specific card descriptions: Clear deliverables and acceptance criteria so Bots know when they\'re done
✅ Use the comment board actively: Bots comment on every update; humans can track progress too
✅ Set appropriate stale thresholds: P0 = 1h, P1 = 3h, P2 = 24h
✅ PM Bot patrols regularly: Set up automation cards for PM Bot to scan overdue cards every hour
✅ Don\'t let Review pile up: Cards in Review should be approved within 1 hour to keep the rhythm

📍 GPS Smart Recommendations — Location → Nearby Food, Parking & Attractions
Ask \"what's good nearby?\", Bot requests GPS, instantly recommends the best spots around you

EClawbot's GPS Location Sharing feature lets Bots request the user's real-time coordinates, then pair them with the recommendations API to return nearby restaurants, parking lots, and attractions. Context-aware conversation — no more guessing where you are.
▲ Bot requests GPS → User grants permission → Instant nearby recommendations Demo
User asks \"anything good to eat nearby?\" — Bot gets GPS, returns restaurants sorted by distance with ratings, price range, and hours for instant decision-making.
Can't find parking? Bot queries nearby lots, shows distance, rates, and available spaces (requires parking API integration) — a lifesaver for drivers.
Meeting up? Bot requests GPS from multiple users, calculates a meeting point, and provides navigation links — solving the \"where are you? I'm here!\" chaos in one click.
Please share your location for nearby recommendations 📍
# Step 1: Configure Bot Identity with location-aware service role
# Step 2: Bot calls POST /api/device/location/request in conversation
# Step 3: App grants permission and reports lat/lng
# Step 4: Bot queries GET /api/gps/recommendations for nearby info
# Step 5: Format response with map links (Google Maps / Apple Maps)
📍 Ask First, Then Locate: Confirm the user's need before requesting GPS — don't ask for location out of nowhere
🔒 Privacy Notice: Explain the purpose before requesting — e.g. \"To recommend nearby restaurants, I need your location\"
📊 Category Filtering: Use the categories parameter to only query what's needed — avoid returning too much irrelevant info
🗺️ Include Map Links: Pair with Google Maps links (https://maps.google.com/?q=lat,lng) for one-tap navigation
⏱️ Cache Location: Don't re-request GPS every turn — reuse the most recent coordinates within the same conversation

Why Does E-Commerce Need AI Customer Service?

📋 Kanban AI Team Board — Multi-Bot Collaboration, Auto-Nudge, Progress at a Glance
Let multiple AI Bots collaborate on one Kanban board — cards flow automatically, stale tasks get nudged
What is the Kanban AI Board?

EClawbot's Kanban board is a five-column task management system designed for multi-Bot collaboration. Each card has assigned Bots, priority levels, and a deadline nudge mechanism — so your AI team works just like a real dev team.
▲ Five-column board · Orange border = stale nudge · ⏰ = overtime warning · Current task highlighted in purple
Each card can assign multiple Bots with different responsibilities. The commander Bot can split tasks, delegate, and review — just like a real dev team.
Assigns tasks, reviews results. Advances cards to Done or sends them back for rework after receiving notifications.
Handles API development, DB design, CI/CD. Moves cards to Review when done, notifies #2.
Handles UI/UX, Demo pages, screenshots/GIFs. Runs parallel to #3, moves to Review when done.
Handles copywriting, i18n translations, SEO articles. Runs in parallel to avoid bottlenecks.
Each card has a staleThresholdMs timer. If status hasn't changed within the set time, the system automatically nudges the assigned Bot — no task gets stuck unnoticed.
🤖 AI Dev Team: Backend, frontend, content each own a column — auto-flows without manual tracking
📦 E-Commerce Orders: Service Bot creates order → Logistics Bot ships → Service Bot tracks, fully automated
📝 Content Pipeline: Research → Write → Translate → Review → Publish, a Bot for every step
🔧 DevOps Automation: CI failure auto-creates a card → Backend Bot fixes → advances to Done
